source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Hand Injury, Well A019 Decompletion

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Garden Banks Block 426A

event.top_event: Hand injury from struck by falling/bouncing tool
actions_taken: H&P SSE floorhand immediately taken to rig hospital for medical care
Evacuation at 1840 hours on 23 November 2021 on Medevac flight
Surgical repair of lacerated tendon to right-hand little finger
22 sutures applied to close wound to right-hand ring finger
Placed on restricted work beginning 24 November 2021 for approximately five days
event.summary: At approximately 1545 hours on 23 November 2021, a Helmerich & Payne short service employee (SSE) floorhand sustained a right-hand injury during Well A019 decompletion operations on the H&P 406 rig at Shell's Auger TLP. While removing a split wear bushing from a running tool using lifting eyes as handles, the bushing dropped approximately eight inches onto a rubber rotary mat, bounced back three inches, and struck the employee's right hand positioned between the bushing and running tool. The employee was evacuated for medical treatment and placed on restricted work for approximately five days.
key_phrases: Job Safety Analysis failed to identify hazard

hazards.name: Pinch point hazard from tool handling
Congested workspace preventing safe tool placement

threats.name: Improper hand placement between tool and running tool
Tool bouncing off rubber rotary mat striking worker
Inadequate work procedure compliance for wear bushing removal

consequences.name: Hand injury - lacerated tendon and lacerations to fingers
Worker evacuation and medical treatment requirement
Restricted work assignment greater than three days

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for decompletion activities
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards associated with tool handling and bouncing
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to identify hazards before work execution

supporting_text: the H&P Job Safety Analysis (JSA) failed to identify that bouncing tools off the rubber rotary mat may pose a hazard

controls.name: TechnipFMC Work Procedure for Decompletion Activity - support lines for lifting eyes
controls.barrier_role: Prevent improper hand placement and tool dropping by using support lines
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to ensure safe tool handling technique

supporting_text: not utilizing support lines attached to the lifting eyes as recommended in the TechnipFMC Work Procedure for Decompletion Activity for removal of wear bushing from running tool

controls.name: Hands-free tools for wear bushing removal
controls.barrier_role: Minimize pinch point hazards and prevent hand placement in danger zone
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to eliminate hand placement in pinch points

supporting_text: not utilizing hands-free tools to minimize the potential for pinch points
not utilizing hands-free tools as recommended to minimize the potential for pinch points

controls.name: Workspace layout management - safe tool placement area
controls.barrier_role: Provide adequate space to safely place tools on floor instead of using hand placement
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to eliminate congested workspace hazard

supporting_text: a jet sub was made up below the wear bushing running tool that prevented the spilt wear bushing to be placed on the floor safely

controls.name: Competency and training for short service employees
controls.barrier_role: Ensure workers have adequate experience and training before performing complex tasks
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure worker competency

supporting_text: H&P SSE on his second hitch as a floorhand
H&P SSE had worked for H&P since 8 February 2021, but this was only his second hitch as a floorhand and he was inexperience doing this task

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
